items.title: Netanyahu says Iran’s uranium enrichment abilities destroyed
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu claims Iran no longer has “the capacity to enrich uranium."
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu claims Iran no longer has “the capacity to enrich uranium."

items.title: Replay: Israeli PM Netanyahu addresses press conference
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday defended Israel’s and the US’s campaign against Iran, saying that airstrikes have largely destroyed Iran’s capacity to enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles. Speaking at a press conference, Netanyahu declared, “We are winning, and Iran is being decimated,” highlighting the ongoing destruction of missile and nuclear weapons production facilities.
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday defended Israel’s and the US’s campaign against Iran, saying that airstrikes have largely destroyed Iran’s capacity to enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles. clusters.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title: Reactions in Dakar

items.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title: Reactions in Dakar
items.standfirst: In a shock, historic decision, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) announced that it is withdrawing the Africa Cup of Nations trophy from Senegal, awarding it instead to Morocco. The decision follows complaints from the host nation Moroccan, after a highly contested final. The news has left many Senegalese in shock, torn between confusion and anger, as FRANCE 24’s team in Dakar reports.
items.content: In a shock, historic decision, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) announced that it is withdrawing the Africa Cup of Nations trophy from Senegal, awarding it instead to Morocco. The decision follows complaints from the host nation Moroccan, after a highly contested final. The news has left many Senegalese in shock, torn between confusion and anger, as FRANCE 24’s team in Dakar reports.

items.title: Senegal vows to appeal 'unfair, unacceptable' ruling stripping it of African football trophy
items.standfirst: Senegal says it will appeal the "unfair, unprecedented, and unacceptable decision" to strip it of its Africa Cup of Nations title after a chaotic final against host Morocco.
items.content: Senegal says it will appeal the "unfair, unprecedented, and unacceptable decision" to strip it of its Africa Cup of Nations title after a chaotic final against host Morocco.

ai_summary: Senegal loses Africa Cup of Nations title

items.content: At the Emergency Hospital, dozens crowded around a thick book to check the names of the victims killed in an airstrike on a rehabilitation center. The U.N. says over a hundred people were killed.(Image credit: Fazelminallah Qazizai for NPR)

items.title: Pakistan to pause Afghan strikes for Eid, two days after deadly Kabul attack
items.standfirst: Five-day cessation announced as mass funeral held for some of hundreds of victims of airstrike on rehab centrePakistan has announced a five-day pause in strikes against neighbouring Afghanistan, as a mass funeral was held for some of the hundreds of victims killed in Monday’s attack on a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul.The Afghan Taliban government has said more than 400 people were killed and 265 others wounded in that attack, which took place as people at the centre were praying days before the end of the holy month of Ramadan. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

ai_summary: Pakistan and Afghanistan agree to truce
Truce announced for Eid celebrations

Pakistan to pause strikes for five days
